route.name: North Bay Overnighter
route.region: Marin Headlands / Mount Tam / Bolinas Ridge
route.description: Official/public GPX overnighter with Marin fire roads, short singletrack pieces, pavement connectors, and Bolinas Ridge conditions.
route.surfaceMix: Official/public GPX overnighter with Marin fire roads, short singletrack pieces, pavement connectors, and Bolinas Ridge conditions.
route.bestSeason: Year-round outside heavy rain and fire-weather closures; refresh camp access for July 10-13.

difficultyProfile.surface: Official/public GPX overnighter with Marin fire roads, short singletrack pieces, pavement connectors, and Bolinas Ridge conditions.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Source notes identify campgrounds and fountains; confirm current water before departure.
resources.food: Fairfax, Lagunitas, Muir Beach/Pelican Inn area, and route-adjacent towns.
resources.camping: Samuel P. Taylor and Pantoll hiker-biker/first-come camping are key anchors; July 11 availability must be confirmed.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Source coverage is now sufficient for Plan Ready, but the actual departure-window overnight, weather, smoke, and closure gates remain unconfirmed.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Bikepacking.com route page lists Download GPX, 72 mi, 2-3 days, 72% unpaved.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: Samuel P. Taylor reservation table shows Fri July 10 and Sat July 11, 2026 as NO; Mount Tam also shows Fri July 10 and Sat July 11 as NO. Hike-bike/Pantoll/Bootjack are first-come and not exact-date confirmable online.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Mount Tamalpais State Park page lists no current trail closures, but final NWS/fire/smoke refresh is still operational and date-specific.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: Mount Tamalpais source confirms bicycles are allowed only on fire roads and Coast View Trail; GPX-level bike legality still needs route audit before gate confirmation.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: Route notes and state-park pages identify fountains/drinking water, but water is tied to camps/park facilities and the overnight plan is not solved.

logistics: Samuel P. Taylor official page last checked July 2, 2026 7:46 AM shows reservation availability NO for Fri July 10 and Sat July 11, YES for Sun July 12 and Mon July 13. https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=469

Samuel P. Taylor page lists hike-and-bike campsites, restrooms/showers, drinking water, and bike trails, but the exact July 11 overnight availability still needs human confirmation. https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=469
Mount Tamalpais State Park source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=471
Samuel P. Taylor State Park source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=469
Subagent GPX/artifact check: public GPX parsed locally with 2 tracks and 4320 trackpoints; terrain map usable.
Official source queue: https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=469 and https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=471
Subagent identified a shortened Fairfax or Marin Headlands to Samuel P. Taylor style overnighter as the only Bay Area thread worth keeping alive.
Any shortened plan needs a backup lodging or transit exit if hiker-bike capacity is full.
Plan Ready pilot source audit checked July 5, 2026 for North Bay Overnighter.

Bailout source: Marin Transit West Marin Stagecoach Routes 61 and 68 are daily, bike-rack-equipped rural routes serving Mt. Tam/Stinson/Bolinas and San Rafael/Fairfax/Lagunitas/Olema/Point Reyes corridors.
Parking source: Mount Tamalpais State Park lists East Peak, Pantoll, and Bootjack parking areas with fees and after-hours rules.

readinessEvidence.finding: Bikepacking.com route page lists Download GPX, 72 mi, 2-3 days, 72% unpaved, and states Samuel P. Taylor hiker-biker is the best overnight near halfway.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: BIKEPACKING.com North Bay Overnighter

readinessEvidence.finding: Samuel P. Taylor reservation availability table says two-night starts Fri 7/10/26 NO and Sat 7/11/26 NO; Sun 7/12/26 and Mon 7/13/26 YES. Hike & Bike is first-come, first-served, max 8 persons, so it is not exact date-confirmable online.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor

readinessEvidence.finding: Official route source identifies the public route geometry and route identity for the North Bay Overnighter.
